qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary Groovy code execution on Jenkins controller via matrix exclude filter injection in .jervis.yml
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns Groovy expression injection through LifecycleGenerator.matrixExcludeFilterBuilder() and MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy GroovyShell.evaluate(); the advisory concerns deterministic AES IV derivation in SecurityIO.groovy encryption/decryption helpers.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described deterministic AES IV derivation in net.gleske.jervis.tools.SecurityIO.groovy, where the same passphrase and plaintext produce the same ciphertext due to IV reuse. The only qualifying finding identifies an unrelated GroovyShell code injection path in matrix exclude/include filtering.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-crxp-chh4-9ghp cites SecurityIO.groovy encryption/decryption code and states that same passphrase plus same plaintext yields same ciphertext because the IV is deterministically derived.

groovy_rce_via_matrix_exclude_filter/vulnerability_report.json cites LifecycleGenerator.groovy lines 561, 564, and 571 and MultiPlatformGenerator.groovy line 526 for string interpolation into GroovyShell.evaluate().
The qualifying finding's root cause, affected files, sink, vulnerability class, and security consequence do not overlap with SecurityIO.groovy AES IV generation or ciphertext layout.
